items.title: Bootstrap Claude Code from Any Harness
items.description: Reverse sync: read an existing Cursor, Codex, or Aider config and generate a CLAUDE.md + skills scaffold. Solves the cold-start problem for new users who already have years of config in another harness and don't want to start from scratch in Claude Code.

items.title: Capability Gap Report
items.description: After sync, produce a ranked list of Claude Code features (skills, MCP tools, hooks) that couldn't be faithfully represented in each target. Shows users exactly what they lose when working in Codex vs Gemini CLI vs Windsurf, so they can make informed harness choices per task.

items.title: Harness-Specific Override Blocks
items.description: Syntax in CLAUDE.md to annotate sections as target-specific (e.g., `<!-- harness:codex-only -->` or `<!-- harness:skip:gemini -->`). Lets power users maintain one source file with per-harness customizations instead of managing exceptions outside the file.

items.title: Config Inheritance: Personal → Team → Org
items.description: A layered config model where personal rules extend team rules which extend org-wide rules. HarnessSync merges the layers and syncs the resolved result. Solves the pain of teams maintaining a shared CLAUDE.md while individuals can't add their own overrides without polluting the shared config.

items.title: Secret Leak Prevention
items.description: Scan config files for tokens, API keys, and credentials before syncing them to targets. Block the sync and warn the user. Critical for teams using org MCP configs that might accidentally contain service account credentials in connection strings.

items.title: Interactive Sync Preview (Dry Run Mode)
items.description: Show a color-coded diff of exactly what files would be written, created, or deleted before any sync happens. Users currently can't easily see impact before committing. Makes syncing feel safe and reduces fear of overwriting manual edits in target harnesses.

items.title: Guided Conflict Resolution
items.description: When a target file has been manually edited since last sync, instead of overwriting it, show a three-way merge view (source, target, proposed) and let the user choose per-hunk. Solves the most common cause of sync hesitancy: fear of losing manual tweaks made directly in Cursor or Codex.

items.title: Per-Harness Compatibility Score
items.description: A 0–100 score showing how faithfully your Claude Code config translates to each target harness. Tracks over time as your config grows or as harnesses add features. Helps users understand ROI of investing in Claude Code-specific features.

items.title: CI/CD Sync Verification Action
items.description: A GitHub Actions / GitLab CI job that runs after config changes and fails the build if any target harness config is out of sync or invalid. Enforces team standards and catches drift before it reaches production. Essential for teams with multiple contributors touching AI config.

items.title: Visual Sync Timeline
items.description: A timeline view showing every sync event with timestamp, targets synced, files changed, and what triggered it (manual vs hook vs schedule). Lets users audit what happened, correlate config changes with behavior changes in their AI harnesses, and understand the sync history at a glance.

items.title: Auto-Generate Config Documentation
items.description: Export your full Claude Code setup (skills, commands, MCP servers, rules) as a formatted Markdown or HTML document. Useful for onboarding teammates, writing internal wikis, or publishing your setup publicly. The skills are already structured — this surfaces that structure for humans.

items.title: Sync Config Templates
items.description: Curated starter templates for common setups: 'Python backend dev', 'React frontend', 'Data science', 'DevOps'. Each template ships with pre-configured rules, recommended MCPs, and sync settings. Lowers the barrier to entry for new users who don't know where to start.

items.title: MCP Server Health Dashboard
items.description: Live status panel showing which MCP servers are reachable from each target harness. Highlights mismatches where an MCP works in Claude Code but the synced config for Cursor points to a non-existent or unreachable server. Saves debugging time when tools silently fail.

items.title: Target Harness Version Pinning
items.description: Pin sync output to a specific version of each harness's config format (e.g., 'Cursor 0.43 schema'). When harnesses update their formats, HarnessSync doesn't break existing setups until the user explicitly upgrades. Critical for teams that can't risk a sudden format migration.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Save named sync configurations (e.g., 'work-laptop', 'open-source-mode', 'client-project') that specify different targets, section filters, and MCP sets. Switch profiles with a single command. Solves the multi-context problem where you need different configs for different roles.

items.title: Remote Machine Sync over SSH
items.description: Sync your Claude Code config to a remote development machine via SSH. Specify a remote host and path; HarnessSync copies and transforms config to that machine. Solves the pain of keeping AI configs in sync between laptop and a powerful cloud dev box.

items.title: Adapter Format Change Detector
items.description: Periodically check if target harnesses have released new versions with config format changes. Alert the user when an adapter may be producing outdated output. Prevents silent incompatibilities where synced config is valid but ignored by a newer harness version.

items.title: Rule Effectiveness Annotations
items.description: Let users annotate rules with effectiveness notes ('this rule reduced hallucinations in Go code by ~40%') that persist in CLAUDE.md comments. These annotations propagate to all targets as comments. Helps teams share institutional knowledge about why rules exist.

items.title: MCP Routing Rules
items.description: Define which MCP servers sync to which harnesses (e.g., 'sentry MCP only to Claude Code and Cursor, not Aider'). Prevents exposing internal tooling to harnesses used for open-source work or that have different trust boundaries.

items.title: First-Time Setup Wizard
items.description: An interactive `/sync-setup` walkthrough that detects installed harnesses, discovers existing configs, presents a preview of what will be synced, and guides the user through first-sync configuration. Eliminates the intimidation of configuring sync manually from scratch.

items.title: Cross-Harness Skill Search
items.description: Search across all synced harness configs to find where a skill, rule, or command landed (or didn't). Answers: 'Did my /deploy-review skill make it to Gemini CLI?' Saves time hunting through multiple directories when debugging why a skill doesn't work in a target harness.

items.title: Config Dependency Visualizer
items.description: Render a visual graph of how your config components relate: which skills reference which MCPs, which rules depend on which tools, which commands call which agents. Helps users understand the impact of removing or changing a component before doing so.

items.title: Sync Event Notifications
items.description: Post sync completion notifications to Slack, Discord, or macOS Notification Center. Include a summary: targets updated, files changed, warnings. Useful for team setups where one person manages shared AI config and needs to signal teammates that a refresh has landed.

items.title: Harness Migration Path Planner
items.description: If a user is switching primary harnesses (e.g., moving from Claude Code to Cursor), generate a step-by-step migration plan showing what will transfer cleanly, what needs manual adaptation, and what will be lost. Positions HarnessSync as the tool for harness transitions, not just ongoing sync.

items.title: Sync Anomaly Detection
items.description: Flag unusual sync events: a target file grew 10x larger than the source, a previously synced skill is now missing, or a target config was last synced 30+ days ago. Proactively surfaces problems the user might not notice until something breaks in a coding session.

items.title: Rule Tagging and Filtering
items.description: Tag rules in CLAUDE.md with metadata (#security, #python, #frontend, #experimental) and configure sync to only push rules with specific tags to specific targets. Enables fine-grained control without duplicating source files — one CLAUDE.md with tags, many filtered outputs.

items.title: Shadow Mode (Write to Temp, Diff Only)
items.description: A mode where HarnessSync writes to temporary files and surfaces diffs without touching real target configs. Useful for evaluating what sync *would* do before enabling it in a production setup, or for teams that review changes before applying.

items.title: Sync Plugin Manifests Across Harnesses
items.description: Translate Claude Code plugin concepts to equivalent structures in target harnesses (Cursor extensions, Gemini plugins). Not a perfect 1:1 map, but syncs what's portable: instructions, tool lists, MCP configs. Lets plugin authors reach multi-harness users automatically.

items.title: A/B Config Testing Across Harnesses
items.description: Define two rule variants and assign them to different harnesses for a period. After a week, `/sync-compare` surfaces which variant felt better (based on edit frequency, tool invocation patterns, user annotations). Brings data-driven iteration to AI config tuning.

items.title: Config Change Attribution
items.description: Tag each sync with the git author and commit that changed the source config. When a target config produces unexpected behavior, users can trace exactly who changed what rule and when. Brings git blame ergonomics to AI harness configuration.

items.title: Live Reload Watch Mode
items.description: A `/sync-watch` mode that monitors Claude Code config files and syncs targets within 1-2 seconds of any change — without requiring a hook to fire. Useful when editing CLAUDE.md directly in an editor outside Claude Code, where PostToolUse hooks don't trigger.

items.title: Multi-Project Sync Sweep
items.description: Sync user-level config across all local projects in one command: walk all git repos, apply project-scoped rules where applicable. Solves the problem of project configs drifting after a harness is installed long after the project was created.

items.title: Sync Coverage Badge for READMEs
items.description: Generate a badge (like a test coverage badge) showing how many harnesses your project config is synced to: `AI Harness Coverage: 5/6`. Useful for open-source projects that want to signal multi-harness support and for teams tracking adoption of shared config standards.

items.title: Harness Config Changelog Generator
items.description: After each sync, auto-generate a human-readable changelog entry: 'Added 3 rules, updated MCP config for Sentry, removed deprecated Aider flag.' Accumulate these into a SYNC_CHANGELOG.md. Useful for teams auditing what changed in AI config over time without reading diffs.

items.title: Environment-Aware Sync (Dev / Staging / Prod)
items.description: Define environment-specific sync rules: dev machines get all MCPs including internal tools, CI gets a restricted read-only set, staging gets production-equivalent rules. Prevents accidental exposure of dev-only tooling in production AI assistants.

items.title: Team Config Server (Central Truth)
items.description: A lightweight HTTP server mode where one machine hosts the canonical config and all other team machines pull from it on sync. Eliminates the git-commit-to-sync-config loop for teams — config changes propagate to all teammates' harnesses within minutes.

items.title: Rule Quality Linter
items.description: Analyze rules in CLAUDE.md for common quality issues: vague instructions ('be helpful'), conflicting rules, rules that reference non-existent tools, or rules too long for target harnesses' context windows. Improves rule effectiveness across all harnesses, not just Claude Code.

items.title: Deprecated Feature Warnings
items.description: Detect when synced config uses patterns that are deprecated or removed in the installed version of a target harness (e.g., an old Cursor MDC key that was renamed). Surface actionable warnings before the user hits a confusing silent failure in the field.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
